lesson.title: Discrete Random Variables
lesson.body: A discrete random variable X assigns a real number to each outcome in Ω, taking values in a countable set. Its probability mass function (PMF) p(x) = P(X = x) satisfies p(x) ≥ 0 and Σp(x) = 1. The CDF is F(x) = P(X ≤ x) = Σ_{t≤x} p(t). The expected value E[X] = Σ x·p(x) and variance Var(X) = E[X²] − (E[X])² measure center and spread.

rooms.question: A random variable X has PMF: p(1) = 0.2, p(2) = 0.5, p(3) = 0.3. What is E[X]?
For the same PMF — p(1) = 0.2, p(2) = 0.5, p(3) = 0.3 — compute Var(X).
Which of the following is a valid PMF for a random variable taking values {0, 1, 2}?
steps: Check that all values are non-negative.
Check that they sum to exactly 1.
rooms.explanation: Option A: 0.1+0.6+0.3 = 1.0 with all values ≥ 0 — valid. Option B sums to 1.1. Option C has p(0) < 0. Option D sums to 0.9.

rooms.question: A fair coin is flipped 3 times. Let X = number of heads. What is P(X ≥ 2)?
Order these steps to compute Var(X) using the formula Var(X) = E[X²] − (E[X])²:
rooms.explanation: First compute E[X], then compute E[X²], then square E[X], and finally subtract to get Var(X).

boss.name: The Discrete Oracle
boss.taunt: Every outcome has a weight. Yours... is negligible.

questions.question: X has PMF p(x) = c·x for x = 1, 2, 3, 4. Find the constant c.
If X and Y are independent random variables, which identity holds for Var(X + Y)?
questions.explanation: For independent X and Y, Cov(X,Y) = 0, so Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y). The general formula includes 2Cov(X,Y), which vanishes under independence.

questions.question: X has PMF p(x) = 1/10 · x for x = 1,2,3,4 (from the prior question). Compute E[X].
